sections.title: Undead Asylum
sections.content:
# Undead Asylum

The Undead Asylum is the tutorial area of Dark Souls Remastered and the first location you will explore. It is a crumbling hilltop prison where the Undead are locked away to await the end of the world. Despite its brevity, the Asylum teaches you everything you need to know about Dark Souls: exploration, caution, combat timing, and the crushing weight of a boss encounter.

<!-- checkpoint: cell-escape | Escape the Starting Cell -->

## Escape the Starting Cell

You awaken in a dank cell deep within the Asylum. The opening cinematic has told you of the Undead prophecy and the Chosen Undead — that's you, though you don't look like much right now. Your cell is bare except for a corpse slumped against the far wall.

Loot the **Dungeon Cell Key** from the corpse. This is your ticket out. Use it on the cell door and step into the corridor beyond. The hallway is lined with more cells, all sealed shut. A few hollowed prisoners claw at the bars but cannot reach you.

Proceed forward and up the short staircase. On the right side of the steps you'll find another corpse holding the **Straight Sword Hilt** — a broken weapon that deals pitiful damage, but it's all you have for now. Pick it up; you'll need something to swing.

At the top of the stairs you emerge into a small courtyard open to the sky. There is a **bonfire** here — your first. Light it and rest. This is Dark Souls' checkpoint system: resting refills your Estus Flask (once you have it), resets most enemies, and serves as your respawn point on death.

<!-- checkpoint: first-bonfire | Light the First Bonfire -->

## The Large Hall & First Demon Encounter

From the bonfire courtyard, proceed through the large double doors ahead. You enter an enormous hall with a vaulted ceiling. The floor is littered with rubble and broken pillars.

As you move toward the center of the room, the **Asylum Demon** crashes down from above — a massive, bloated creature wielding a stone hammer the size of a wagon. With only the Straight Sword Hilt (or bare fists), you deal almost no damage to this beast.

**Do NOT fight it.** Instead, look to the far-left wall. There is a small doorway — sprint toward it. The Demon's overhead slam has a long wind-up, so roll through its attack and dash through the door. A fog gate seals behind you, and you're safe.

If you are experienced and chose the **Black Firebombs** as your starting gift, you can actually kill the Asylum Demon here for the **Demon's Great Hammer** — but this is an advanced strategy not recommended for first-time players.

<!-- checkpoint: oscar-encounter | Meet Oscar of Astora -->

## The Refuge Hallway & Oscar of Astora

Beyond the fog gate is a narrow hallway leading upward. Light the **second bonfire** partway up the corridor. Continue climbing the stairs.

**Boulder Trap:** As you ascend the next staircase, a massive boulder rolls down toward you. Sprint back or dodge sideways. The boulder smashes through the wall behind you, revealing a hidden alcove.

Inside this alcove lies **Oscar, Knight of Astora** — mortally wounded and slumped against the wall. Speak with him. Oscar recognizes you as Undead and gifts you the **Estus Flask** (your primary healing item, refills at bonfires) and the **Undead Asylum F2 East Key**.

Oscar speaks of a prophecy: *"Thou who art Undead, art chosen. In thine exodus from the Undead Asylum, maketh pilgrimage to the land of Ancient Lords."* He then slumps over. He will hollow later — if you return to this room after defeating the boss, he attacks on sight and drops the **Crest Shield**.

<!-- checkpoint: retrieve-equipment | Retrieve Your Equipment -->

## Retrieve Your Starting Equipment

With the F2 East Key in hand, proceed up past the boulder trap area. At the top of the stairs, unlock the gate on the right side. Beyond it is a narrow corridor lined with hollow soldiers — slow, shambling enemies that attack with broken swords.

These are your first real combat encounters. Practice your timing: raise your shield to block, then counter-attack. Or try rolling through their swings for backstab opportunities.

Along this upper walkway, you will find:

- Your **class shield** on a corpse along the railing
- Your **class weapon** at the end of the corridor (the weapon depends on what class you chose at character creation)
- A few **Soul of a Lost Undead** items on scattered corpses

There are also **hollow archers** on elevated platforms. They fire slowly but can stagger you mid-fight. Prioritize closing the distance or using pillars as cover.

Equip your weapon and shield immediately. You are now properly armed.

<!-- checkpoint: fog-gate-return | Return to the Demon Arena -->

## Return to the Demon Arena

With your real weapon equipped, it's time to face the Asylum Demon properly. Make your way back through the upper corridors until you reach a fog gate overlooking the large hall from above. You are now on a ledge directly above the Asylum Demon.

Before dropping down, ensure your Estus Flask is equipped and your weapon is two-handed (if you prefer damage over blocking). Take a breath. Step off the ledge.

<!-- checkpoint: asylum-demon-boss | Boss: Asylum Demon -->

## Boss Fight: Asylum Demon

**Asylum Demon** is a large, slow creature with devastating but highly telegraphed attacks.

### Plunging Attack

As you drop from the ledge, press your attack button (R1/RB) while falling. This executes a **plunging attack** that deals roughly 30-40% of the Demon's health in one strike. This is by far the most efficient opening.

### Attack Patterns

- **Overhead Slam:** The Demon raises its hammer high and crashes it down. Huge damage, but very slow. Roll sideways.
- **Horizontal Sweep:** A wide arc in front of the Demon. Roll backward or toward its legs.
- **Butt Slam (AoE):** The Demon leaps and lands on its backside, creating a shockwave. Sprint away when you see it jump.
- **Fly & Slam:** At range, the Demon can briefly fly toward you and slam. Roll sideways on landing.

### Strategy

After the plunging attack, run behind the Demon and stay near its tail/legs. Most of its attacks swing in front, so the rear is safest. Get in 1-2 hits after each of its attack animations, then back off. Be patient — greed kills more players than the Demon itself.

Heal with Estus when below 50% HP. You have 5 charges; that's more than enough if you play carefully.

**Reward:** Defeating the Asylum Demon grants you the **Big Pilgrim's Key** and a large chunk of souls.

<!-- checkpoint: exit-asylum | Exit the Asylum -->

## Exit the Asylum

With the Big Pilgrim's Key, exit through the massive iron doors at the far end of the hall (opposite from where you first entered). A short path leads you outside to a cliff edge overlooking a vast landscape.

Step forward onto the rocky promontory. After a moment, the **Giant Crow** swoops down, grasps you in its talons, and carries you away from the Asylum. A cinematic plays showing you soaring over the land of Lordran.

You are deposited at **Firelink Shrine** — the central hub of Dark Souls. The real journey begins now.

*Note: You can return to the Undead Asylum later in the game by curling up in the crow's nest at Firelink Shrine. The return trip features new enemies, the Stray Demon boss, and additional loot including the Rusted Iron Ring.*

steps.text: Use the key to unlock the cell door and exit into the corridor.

steps.note: A broken weapon — weak but better than bare fists.

The **Asylum Demon** drops from the ceiling. Do NOT fight it — sprint to the far-left doorway and through the fog gate.

Receive the **Estus Flask** from Oscar — your primary healing item (refills at bonfires, starts with 5 charges).

Use the F2 East Key to unlock the gate at the top of the stairs and proceed into the upper corridor.
Defeat the **hollow soldiers** in the corridor. Practice blocking and counter-attacking.

Pick up your **class weapon** at the end of the corridor.
steps.note: The weapon depends on your chosen starting class.

steps.text: Collect **Soul of a Lost Undead** items from corpses scattered along the upper walkway.
Watch for **hollow archers** on elevated platforms. Use pillars as cover or close the distance quickly.
Equip your class weapon and shield from the inventory menu.
Navigate back through the upper corridors to the fog gate overlooking the large hall from above.

**BOSS: Asylum Demon** — Drop from the ledge and press attack while falling for a devastating **plunging attack** (deals ~35% of its health). Stay behind the Demon, attack 1-2 times after each of its swings, and heal below 50% HP.
steps.note: Reward: Big Pilgrim's Key. Avoid greed — wait for openings after its overhead slam and horizontal sweep.

steps.text: Receive the **Big Pilgrim's Key** upon defeating the Asylum Demon.
Use the Big Pilgrim's Key to open the massive iron doors and walk to the cliff edge. The Giant Crow carries you to Firelink Shrine.
Optional: Return to Oscar's alcove after the boss fight. He has hollowed and attacks on sight. Defeat him for the **Crest Shield**.
steps.note: This is missable if you leave the Asylum without returning.

sections.title: Firelink Shrine
sections.content:
# Firelink Shrine

Firelink Shrine is the central hub of Dark Souls Remastered. Nestled among ancient ruins overlooking a flooded basin, it serves as your home base throughout the entire game. Multiple paths branch outward from here to the various regions of Lordran, and friendly NPCs gradually congregate at the Shrine as you progress.

The Shrine's bonfire is unique — it is tended by a **Fire Keeper**, which means it provides more Estus charges than a standard bonfire (10 instead of 5). This makes it an ideal place to return between expeditions.

<!-- checkpoint: arrive-firelink | Arrive at Firelink Shrine -->

## Arrival

The Giant Crow deposits you on a grassy cliff above the Shrine. Descend the short slope to find the **Firelink bonfire** — a crackling flame surrounded by ancient stonework. Rest here to set your spawn point and refill your Estus Flask (you now receive 10 charges thanks to the Fire Keeper).

Take a moment to survey the area. Firelink Shrine is a ruined plaza built into a hillside, with stairways leading up and down in several directions. The sky is grey and overcast. The sound of distant bells and dripping water fills the air.

<!-- checkpoint: meet-crestfallen | Meet the Crestfallen Warrior -->

## NPCs at the Shrine

### Crestfallen Warrior

Sitting on the stone steps near the bonfire is the **Crestfallen Warrior** — a cynical, world-weary Undead in chain mail. Speak with him for invaluable early-game guidance. He tells you about the two Bells of Awakening — one above in the Undead Church, one below in Blighttown — and warns you about the dangers ahead.

He's sarcastic and dismissive, but his information is accurate. Exhaust his dialogue on each visit; he updates his lines as you progress.

### Anastacia of Astora (Fire Keeper)

Below the bonfire, accessible via a staircase behind and beneath the main platform, you'll find **Anastacia of Astora** imprisoned behind iron bars. She is the Fire Keeper who tends this bonfire. She cannot speak (her tongue was removed) but plays a vital role:

- She keeps the Firelink bonfire lit
- She can reinforce your Estus Flask if you bring her **Fire Keeper Souls** (found throughout the game)
- If she dies, the Firelink bonfire goes dark until you avenge her

Do NOT attack her. She is essential.

### Petrus of Thorolund

To the right of the bonfire (when facing the cliff edge), descend a few steps to find **Petrus of Thorolund** — a portly cleric who serves as your first Miracle merchant. He sells basic miracles like **Heal** and offers to teach you about Covenants.

He later plays a role in the Rhea of Thorolund questline. For now, buy **Heal** if you're running a Faith build.

### Other NPCs (Arrive Later)

As you progress through the game, additional NPCs will appear at Firelink Shrine:

- **Knight Lautrec of Carim** — freed from Undead Parish, sits near the bonfire. Treacherous.
- **Laurentius of the Great Swamp** — rescued from the Depths. Pyromancy trainer.
- **Griggs of Vinheim** — rescued from Lower Undead Burg. Sorcery merchant.
- **Siegmeyer of Catarina** — appears at various points in his questline.
- **Kingseeker Frampt** — the primordial serpent, appears after ringing both Bells of Awakening.

<!-- checkpoint: explore-items | Explore for Items -->

## Item Locations

Firelink Shrine contains a surprising number of useful items scattered across its various levels and ledges. Explore thoroughly before venturing onward.

### Near the Bonfire

- **Homeward Bone x6** — on a corpse between the bonfire and the elevator tower (leads to New Londo Ruins)
- **Soul of a Lost Undead** — on a corpse near the cliff edge behind the bonfire

### The Well & Surrounding Area

- **Humanity** — on a corpse near the stone well to the left of the bonfire
- **Twin Humanities** — behind the well arch, partially hidden in shadow
- **Soul of a Lost Undead** — on a corpse on the ledge above the well (accessible from the aqueduct path)

### Elevator Tower Area

- **Binoculars** — on a corpse along the cliff edge near the elevator down to New Londo Ruins. Useful for scoping out areas from a distance.

### The Graveyard (Southwest)

Descending the slope past Petrus leads to the **Graveyard** — a dangerous area filled with skeletons that reassemble after being destroyed (unless you have a divine weapon). Despite the danger, several valuable items are here:

- **Winged Spear** — on a corpse among the first few graves
- **Soul items** — scattered on corpses throughout
- **Zweihander** — a massive ultra-greatsword on a corpse near the large gravestone at the bottom of the hill
- **Binoculars** — if you missed them near the elevator

**Warning:** The skeletons here are far too strong for a new character. Grab items by sprinting in, looting, and sprinting out. Do NOT try to clear the graveyard at this stage.

### New Londo Ruins (Below)

The elevator near the bonfire descends to New Londo Ruins. This flooded ghost city is extremely dangerous early on (ghosts are invincible without Transient Curses). However, just past the initial bridge you can find:

- **Fire Keeper Soul** — on a corpse on a rooftop in the ruins. This can be given to Anastacia to upgrade your Estus Flask.

Grab it and immediately return to the elevator. Do NOT explore further until much later in the game.

<!-- checkpoint: understand-routes | Understand the Routes -->

## Routes from Firelink Shrine

Firelink Shrine connects to several areas. Understanding your options early prevents wasted time and frustration.

### Undead Burg (Recommended First)

Climb the staircase that leads up from the bonfire area, past the aqueduct waterway. This leads to the **Undead Burg** — the intended first area after the Asylum. Enemies here are manageable and the path leads logically toward the first Bell of Awakening.

### New Londo Ruins (Dangerous — Later)

Take the elevator down from the tower near the bonfire. New Londo Ruins is populated by ghosts that require **Transient Curses** to damage. This is a mid-to-late-game area.

### The Catacombs (Dangerous — Later)

Descend through the Graveyard and continue downhill. The Catacombs are the path to Nito and the second Bell of Awakening (Blighttown is actually the intended route to the second bell for new players).

### Valley of Drakes (Requires Key)

A locked gate near the New Londo elevator leads to the Valley of Drakes. You need the **Master Key** (starting gift or found later) to access this shortcut.

<!-- checkpoint: level-up-prep | Prepare for Undead Burg -->

## Preparing for Undead Burg

Before leaving Firelink Shrine, make sure you have:

1. **Rested at the bonfire** — full 10 Estus charges
2. **Equipped your best weapon and shield** from the Asylum
3. **Spoken to the Crestfallen Warrior** — he confirms your path: "There are actually two Bells of Awakening... one up above, in the Undead Church, and one far, far below, in the ruins at the base of Blighttown."
4. **Collected nearby items** — at minimum the Humanity and Homeward Bones
5. **Optionally purchased Heal from Petrus** — if running a Faith build

When ready, ascend the stairs toward the aqueduct. The path to Undead Burg awaits.

<!-- checkpoint: depart-firelink | Depart for Undead Burg -->

## Lore Notes

Firelink Shrine was once a place of great significance — a nexus where the First Flame was linked by past heroes. The bonfire here burns with unusual intensity because of Anastacia's sacrifice. The ruins suggest a once-grand structure, now eroded by untold centuries.

The name itself — "Firelink" — refers to the act of linking the First Flame, the central choice that defines the game's ending. Every Lord of Cinder before you began their journey from a place like this.

As you progress, watch how the Shrine changes: NPCs arrive and depart, the atmosphere shifts, and the consequences of your choices ripple outward from this quiet, grey hub at the center of the world.

steps.text: Rest at the **Firelink bonfire** to set your spawn point and refill Estus (10 charges from the Fire Keeper).
Talk to the **Crestfallen Warrior** sitting on the steps near the bonfire. He explains the two Bells of Awakening.
steps.note: Exhaust his dialogue — he gives critical guidance about where to go next.

steps.text: **Anastacia of Astora** (Fire Keeper) is imprisoned below the bonfire behind iron bars. She reinforces your Estus Flask with Fire Keeper Souls.
steps.note: Do NOT attack her — if she dies, the Firelink bonfire goes out.

steps.text: Speak with **Petrus of Thorolund** to the right of the bonfire (down a few steps). He sells miracles and explains Covenants.
steps.note: Buy Heal if you're a Faith build.

steps.text: Pick up **Homeward Bone x6** from the corpse between the bonfire and the elevator tower.
Grab the **Humanity** from the corpse near the stone well (left of the bonfire).
Pick up **Twin Humanities** from behind the well arch, partially hidden in shadow.
Collect **Soul of a Lost Undead** from the corpse near the cliff edge behind the bonfire.
Grab the **Binoculars** from the corpse along the cliff edge near the New Londo elevator.
steps.note: Useful for scoping out areas and sniping with bows or spells.

steps.text: The **Graveyard** (southwest, past Petrus) has powerful skeletons that reassemble. Do NOT try to clear it at this stage.
steps.note: You can sprint in to grab the Winged Spear and Zweihander, then sprint out.

steps.text: Sprint into the Graveyard and grab the **Winged Spear** from a corpse among the first graves.
steps.note: Immediately run back — the skeletons will overwhelm you.

steps.text: Optional: Sprint to the large gravestone at the bottom of the hill for the **Zweihander** (powerful ultra-greatsword).
steps.note: Very risky at low level. Consider coming back later.

steps.text: Take the elevator down to New Londo Ruins and grab the **Fire Keeper Soul** from the corpse on the rooftop just past the first bridge.
steps.note: Give this to Anastacia to upgrade your Estus Flask. Return to the elevator immediately after.

steps.text: Do NOT explore New Londo Ruins beyond the Fire Keeper Soul. Ghosts here require **Transient Curses** to damage and are deadly at low level.
Ensure you have: full 10 Estus, your best weapon and shield equipped, and nearby items collected.
Climb the staircase leading up from the bonfire area, past the aqueduct, to reach **Undead Burg** — the recommended first area.
steps.note: This is the path toward the first Bell of Awakening.

sections.title: Prologue
sections.content:
## Prologue — The Scarlet Night

The game opens with a peaceful flashback to the village of **Aball**, where a young Velvet Crowe lives with her brother Laphicet and brother-in-law Arthur (Artorius). This idyllic scene establishes the bonds that will drive Velvet’s revenge throughout the entire story. Pay attention to the dialogue here — it sets up critical plot points.

<!-- checkpoint: prologue-village | Village of Aball flashback -->

After the opening flashback, the scene shifts dramatically. Velvet awakens in a dark prison cell on the island fortress of **Titania**, where she has been imprisoned for three years. A mysterious Malak named **Seres** appears and frees Velvet from her cell. The game introduces basic movement and camera controls during this sequence.

### Tutorial Battle

Your first fight is against a daemon in the prison cell. The game walks you through the basic combat system:

- **Normal attacks** chain into combos (up to 3 hits initially)
- **Artes** are mapped to face buttons during combat
- **Break Soul** is Velvet’s unique Therionization mechanic — when prompted, activate it to consume enemies and heal

<!-- checkpoint: prologue-tutorial | Tutorial battle completed -->

This daemon fight cannot be lost and serves purely as a combat tutorial. Use Break Soul when the prompt appears to learn Velvet’s signature ability — her left arm transforms into a daemonic claw that devours enemies.

### Following Seres

After the tutorial, follow Seres through the prison hallways. The path is completely linear at this point. Seres will blast through locked doors and handle most obstacles. Along the way, you’ll encounter your first proper enemy fights against prison guards (exorcists).

These corridor battles are straightforward — use basic combos and don’t worry about resource management. Seres fights alongside you as a guest party member and contributes significant damage.

<!-- checkpoint: prologue-escape | Escaped the prison cell -->

The prologue concludes with major story revelations and sets Velvet on her path of vengeance. Watch the cutscenes carefully — they establish the central conflict of the entire game.

steps.text: The game opens with a flashback introducing Velvet and her village of Aball. Follow the tutorial prompts.
Complete the tutorial battle against the daemon in Velvet’s prison cell.
steps.note: Use Break Soul when prompted to learn Velvet’s Therionization mechanic.

steps.text: Follow Seres through the prison hallways after escaping the cell.
Defeat the guards in the initial corridor encounters.
steps.note: These are easy fights to practice basic combos.

sections.title: Titania (1st Visit)
sections.content:
## Titania — Prison Island Escape

After the prologue events, Velvet must fight her way through the Titania prison fortress alongside Seres. This section introduces deeper combat mechanics and features your first real boss encounter.

### Boss: Seres

Immediately after regaining control, you face **Seres** in a tutorial boss fight.

- **HP:** 2,200
- **Strategy:** Guard to accumulate Souls, then counterattack with full combos
- **Reward:** Prodigy’s Glacite

<!-- checkpoint: titania-seres-boss | Defeated Seres (tutorial boss) -->

This fight cannot be lost — it exists to teach you about Soul management and the guard/counter flow of combat. When you guard enemy attacks successfully, you gain Souls which extend your combo length.

### Prison Block Exploration

After the battle, exit the room and follow the linear path forward. Open the first chest you encounter for Velvet’s basic equipment. **Immediately open the party menu and equip everything** — check weapon, armor, and accessory slots.

The prison block has multiple cells and side rooms worth exploring:

- **Northwest cell (last one):** Battle Boots
- **Storage room to the south:** Sage
- **Eastern cell block:** Life Bottle
- **Upstairs northeast area:** Void Ring

<!-- checkpoint: titania-items | Collected prison block items -->

Take your time exploring — enemies here are weak and provide good early experience. Check every cell door and interact with all sparkle points on the ground.

### Upper Floors & Rokurou

Head upstairs and south to encounter exorcist enemies. These are slightly tougher than the guards below but still manageable with basic combos and Break Soul.

<!-- checkpoint: titania-rokurou | Encountered Rokurou -->

### Boss: Red-Eyed Swordsman (Rokurou)

After clearing the upper-floor exorcists, you encounter a prisoner with red eyes — **Rokurou Rangetsu**.

- **HP:** 2,843 (only need to reduce to 50%)
- **Strategy:** Seres assists in this fight. Focus on landing combos when Rokurou finishes his attack strings
- **Reward:** Sharpshooter’s Ventite

<!-- checkpoint: titania-rokurou-boss | Defeated Rokurou -->

Rokurou is fast but predictable — he always follows a three-hit combo pattern. Guard through his attacks, then counter. Once his HP drops to 50%, the fight ends and he joins your escape.

### Escape Sequence

After defeating Rokurou:
1. Defeat the three guards that appear
2. Ascend the ladder and collect **320 Gald** from the chest at the top
3. Watch the concluding cutscenes as the party escapes the prison

<!-- checkpoint: titania-escape | Escaped Titania -->

The escape sequence features important story cutscenes — Seres sacrifices herself, and Velvet gains her full daemonic power. This is a pivotal moment that shapes the rest of the narrative.

steps.text: **BOSS: Seres** — HP: 2,200. Guard to gain Souls, then counterattack.
steps.note: Reward: Prodigy’s Glacite. This is a tutorial boss — you can’t lose.

steps.text: Exit the room after the battle and follow the linear path. Open the chest for Velvet’s basic gear.
Equip Velvet via the party menu.
steps.note: Check all equipment slots — weapon, armor, and accessories.

steps.text: Collect the **Battle Boots** from the last cell in the northwest.
steps.note: Check all cells for items.

steps.text: Pick up the **Sage** from the storage room to the south.
Grab the **Life Bottle** from the eastern cell block.
Find the **Void Ring** upstairs in the northeast area.
Head upstairs and south to encounter exorcist enemies. Defeat them to progress.
**BOSS: Red-Eyed Swordsman (Rokurou)** — HP: 2,843. Only need to drop HP to 50%.
steps.note: Seres assists. Reward: Sharpshooter’s Ventite.

steps.text: Defeat the three guards after the boss fight.
Collect **320 Gald** from the chest after ascending the ladder.
Watch the concluding cutscenes and exit the prison.

sections.title: Figahl Icecaps
sections.content:
## Figahl Icecaps — Frozen Awakening

After escaping Titania, Velvet awakens on the frozen **Figahl Icecaps** with Rokurou and the eccentric witch **Magilou**. The icy landscape stretches before you, and this area serves as your first real exploration zone with proper item gathering and optional content.

### Opening Battle

Immediately upon regaining control, you’re thrown into combat against a **Werewolf** and **Eagle**. These enemies are aggressive but Velvet’s Therionization (Break Soul) makes quick work of them. Use it freely — there’s no penalty for overusing it in these early fights.

<!-- checkpoint: figahl-wake | Awakened on Figahl Icecaps -->

### Exploration & Items

The Figahl Icecaps is a relatively small area but has several collectibles scattered about:

1. **320 Gald** — Turn around immediately after waking up; there’s a copper chest behind your starting position
2. **Life Bottle** — Head north, then veer south to the upper level for a chest
3. **Rosemary** — Field pickup on the left cliff as you head north (permanent stat boost herb)
4. **Katz Box (Ten-Gallon Hat)** — Near the northern exit, after the Katz Souls tutorial

<!-- checkpoint: figahl-items | Collected Figahl Icecaps items -->

### Katz Boxes Tutorial

Partway through the area, you’ll receive a tutorial about the **Katz Box** system. Shiny **Katz Souls** float around the overworld — collect them to spend at Katz Boxes scattered throughout the game. Your first box here costs a small amount and contains the **Ten-Gallon Hat** cosmetic accessory (equip via the Fashion menu, purely cosmetic).

### The Laphicet Lookalike

Continuing north triggers a cutscene where Velvet spots a young boy who looks remarkably like her deceased brother Laphicet. This is a Malak — a spiritual being enslaved by the Abbey. This encounter foreshadows major plot developments.

<!-- checkpoint: figahl-laphicet-scene | Witnessed Laphicet lookalike cutscene -->

After the cutscene, head right. Climb the vines before heading to town to collect a **Sage** herb (another permanent stat boost). Then descend and advance south to reach the port city of **Hellawes**.

**Note:** There is one chest in this area that cannot be reached on your first visit. You can return later once you have the appropriate traversal ability — don’t stress about it now.

<!-- checkpoint: figahl-exit | Reached Hellawes -->

steps.text: After the story scenes, Velvet wakes on the Figahl Icecaps. Defeat the **Werewolf** and **Eagle** in the opening battle.
steps.note: Use Therionization — it makes these fights trivial.

steps.text: Pick up the **Life Bottle** from the chest on the upper level.
Collect **Rosemary** from the field pickup nearby.
steps.note: Permanent stat boost herb — always grab these.

steps.text: Open the chest for **320 Gald** — turn around from your starting position.
You’ll receive a tutorial about **Katz Boxes** — collect shiny Katz Souls on the map to unlock them.
Open the first **Katz Box** for the **Ten-Gallon Hat** accessory.
steps.note: Cosmetic only — equip via the fashion menu.

steps.text: Continue north. After the cutscene with the Laphicet lookalike, head right.
Collect **Sage** by climbing the vines before heading to town.
steps.note: Permanent stat boost herb.

steps.text: Descend and advance to reach **Hellawes**.
One chest in this area is inaccessible on your first visit. Return later with the appropriate traversal ability.

sections.title: Hellawes (1st Visit)
sections.content:
## Hellawes — Port City Under Abbey Control

**Hellawes** is a northern port city firmly under the Abbey’s control. Velvet, Rokurou, and Magilou sneak in through a warehouse on the docks, staying hidden from the exorcists who patrol the streets. This is your first proper town with shops, NPCs, and side content.

### Starting Warehouse

You begin in a warehouse on the docks. Before heading outside, thoroughly search the building:

- **480 Gald** — In the middle of the storehouse
- **Life Bottle** — Northwest corner of the storehouse
- Various cooking ingredients scattered on the floor

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes-warehouse | Explored the starting warehouse -->

### Dock Area

Exit the warehouse and explore the dock area:

- **Fire Ring** — Silver chest in the southeast dock corner (down the steps). **Equip this immediately** for fire resistance
- **Life Bottle** — North part of the docks in a standard chest

The docks have several NPCs with green exclamation marks — talk to them for world-building dialogue and information about the Abbey’s activities in the region.

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes-docks | Explored Hellawes docks -->

### Town Proper & Rooftops

Head into the main town area. Hellawes features accessible rooftops with valuable loot:

- **Lavender** — On the northern rooftops (permanent stat boost)
- **Amber Garment** — Chest on the rooftops accessed via ladders
- **Blood Blade** — Gold chest at the end of the rooftops path (weapon for Rokurou)
- **Amber Fragment** — Hidden area, down the steps from a halfway point on the rooftop
- **Verbana** — Near the merchant’s guild archway (southwest exit area)

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes-rooftops | Collected rooftop treasures -->

### Story Progression

1. **Visit the tavern** to hear about **Victoria**, a powerful Abbey member stationed in the region
2. **Talk to Rokurou** to officially add him to your active party (he’ll be in combat now)
3. **Magilou temporarily leaves** — she can be found lounging in the tavern but won’t rejoin the party yet

### Shopping & Preparation

Visit the shops to upgrade equipment. Key purchases:
- Better weapons for both Velvet and Rokurou
- Armor upgrades (check defense values carefully)
- Stock up on Apple Gels and Life Bottles for the upcoming dungeon

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes-shop | Purchased equipment upgrades -->

**Skit:** Rest at the inn to trigger available skits. The Jacketless Outfit for Rokurou becomes available after resting.

When you’re fully prepared, exit Hellawes toward **Beardsley** via the northern gate.

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes-exit | Left Hellawes toward Beardsley -->

steps.text: Velvet, Rokurou, and Magilou start in a warehouse on the docks. Explore the town for items and story.
Pick up **480 Gald** and a **Life Bottle** in the starting warehouse.
Open the chest in the southeast dock corner for the **Fire Ring**.
steps.note: Equip this immediately — it provides fire resistance.

steps.text: Grab the **Life Bottle** from the north part of the docks.
Find **Lavender** on the northern rooftops.
steps.note: Permanent stat boost herb — always collect these.

steps.text: Collect **Amber Garment** and **Blood Blade** from near the northern rooftops.
Pick up **Verbana** near the merchant’s guild archway.
Find the **Amber Fragment** in the chest behind the inn.
Visit the tavern to hear about **Victoria** (Abbey member).
Talk to Rokurou to officially add him to your active party.
Magilou temporarily leaves — she can be found in the tavern but won’t rejoin yet.
Purchase equipment upgrades from the shop if needed.
steps.note: Prioritize weapons for Velvet and Rokurou.

steps.text: Rest at the inn to trigger skits and unlock the Jacketless Outfit for Rokurou.
Exit Hellawes toward **Beardsley** when ready.

sections.title: Beardsley
sections.content:
## Beardsley — Small Village on the Frontier

**Beardsley** is a small, quiet village nestled between Hellawes and the dangerous Hadlow Hollow cave system. The villagers here live in fear of a daemon named **Dyle** — a lizard-like creature that has been terrorizing the area. This brief town visit is primarily for story setup and restocking before the upcoming dungeon.

### Arrival & NPCs

Upon arriving, explore the village and talk to all NPCs (marked with green exclamation marks). Key information:

- Villagers describe **Dyle**, a powerful daemon hiding in Hadlow Hollow
- The Abbey has been unable (or unwilling) to deal with the daemon threat
- Some NPCs hint at the cave’s layout and dangers

<!-- checkpoint: beardsley-arrival | Arrived in Beardsley -->

### Supplies & Preparation

Visit the shop to stock up before Hadlow Hollow:

- **Apple Gels** (at least 10) — healing is crucial in the upcoming dungeon
- **Life Bottles** (at least 5) — the cave has tough enemies that can KO party members
- **Panacea Bottles** — the cave enemies can inflict poison and paralysis
- Any equipment upgrades available

<!-- checkpoint: beardsley-shop | Stocked up on supplies -->

The enemies in Hadlow Hollow are a step up in difficulty from what you’ve faced so far. Make sure your equipment is current and you have adequate healing items.

### Moving On

When you’re ready, exit the village toward the north to enter **Hadlow Hollow**. Save at the save point near the exit — the cave has limited save opportunities inside.

<!-- checkpoint: beardsley-exit | Headed to Hadlow Hollow -->

steps.text: Arrive in Beardsley and explore the village for items and story events.
Talk to NPCs (green exclamation marks) for information about **Hadlow Hollow** and the daemon Dyle.
Stock up on supplies at the shop before heading into the cave.
steps.note: Buy Apple Gels, Life Bottles, and Panacea Bottles. Cave enemies inflict status ailments.

steps.text: Hadlow Hollow enemies are significantly tougher than previous areas. Ensure equipment is upgraded.
Exit toward **Hadlow Hollow** when ready. Save at the save point near the exit.

sections.title: Hadlow Hollow
sections.content:
## Hadlow Hollow — Daemon’s Den

**Hadlow Hollow** is a dark, daemon-infested cave system. This is your first proper dungeon with branching paths, hidden treasures, environmental hazards (tar pits), and multiple boss encounters. The daemon **Dyle** lurks in the depths, but you’ll also face the **Bat Baron** along the way.

### Entrance & First Fork

Upon entering, you’ll immediately encounter a fork:

- **Northeast path:** Contains an Apple Gel — grab it first
- **West path:** A chest with **500 Gald**

Head west into a large area with roaming enemies. Practice Break Soul mechanics here — the enemies are good training dummies for extended combos.

<!-- checkpoint: hadlow-entrance | Entered Hadlow Hollow -->

### Mid-Dungeon Exploration

The cave is dark but relatively linear with short side branches:

1. **Boulder interaction:** Smash a boulder blocking the northeast path to reveal a **Katz Box** ahead
2. **Tar pit crossing:** Use stepping stones to cross safely — falling in damages your party
3. **East room:** Pick up **Chamomile** (permanent stat boost herb)
4. **Southwest loop:** Collect **Shadow Daggers** for Rokurou (solid weapon upgrade)

Push a boulder into another tar pit to create a new path forward. This environmental puzzle mechanic appears throughout the dungeon.

<!-- checkpoint: hadlow-midpoint | Reached the cave midpoint -->

### Boss: Bat Baron

Midway through the dungeon, you’ll face the **Bat Baron** — your first serious boss fight.

- **HP:** 12,665
- **Weakness:** Elemental Artes (resists neutral/physical attacks)
- **Strategy:**
  - Defeat the smaller bats first to reduce incoming damage
  - Use Velvet’s elemental Artes (Swallow Dance is effective)
  - Break Soul aggressively to maintain HP and extend combos
  - Guard when the Baron charges its dive attack
- **Reward:** Acrobat’s Ventite

<!-- checkpoint: hadlow-bat-baron | Defeated the Bat Baron -->

Don’t be discouraged if this takes a couple of attempts. If you’re struggling, return to the entrance to grind a level or two — the enemies respawn when you re-enter areas.

### Deeper Passages

After the Bat Baron:
- Collect a **Life Bottle** from the chest ahead
- A skit triggers — watch for character development
- Pick up **Sage** (permanent stat boost)
- Further ahead: **530 Gald** in a chest

### Boss: Dyle

At the deepest point of the cave, you confront **Dyle** — the lizard daemon terrorizing Beardsley.

- **HP:** 13,328
- **Weakness:** Exploit Weak Point combos (the game introduces this mechanic here)
- **Strategy:**
  - Dyle has powerful tail swipe attacks — stay to his front or sides
  - Use combos that target his elemental weakness
  - Break Soul to recover when he lands heavy hits
  - Keep healing items ready for his rage phase at low HP

<!-- checkpoint: hadlow-dyle | Defeated Dyle -->

After the fight, an important story scene plays. Velvet spares Dyle under certain conditions, and the party discusses their next move. This is also where the party’s path toward rescuing **Laphicet** (the Malak boy) becomes clear.

### Exiting the Cave

Loop around collecting any treasures you missed, then exit Hadlow Hollow. Your next destination is back to **Hellawes** for a confrontation with the Abbey.

<!-- checkpoint: hadlow-exit | Exited Hadlow Hollow -->

steps.text: Enter Hadlow Hollow and fight through the daemon-infested cave.
Explore all side paths before progressing — several chests are easy to miss in the dark.
Collect **500 Gald** from the west path and **Apple Gel** from the northeast at the first fork.
Open the **Katz Box** after smashing the boulder on the northeast path.
Pick up **Chamomile** in the east room and **Shadow Daggers** (Rokurou weapon) from the southwest loop.
Follow the cave path, pushing boulders into tar pits to create new paths forward.
**BOSS: Bat Baron** — HP: 12,665. Resists physical; use elemental Artes.
steps.note: Defeat smaller bats first. Use Swallow Dance and Break Soul aggressively. Reward: Acrobat’s Ventite.

steps.text: After the Bat Baron, collect a **Life Bottle**, **Sage**, and **530 Gald** from chests ahead.
**BOSS: Dyle** — HP: 13,328. Exploit Weak Point combos introduced in this fight.
steps.note: Stay to his front/sides to avoid tail swipes. Heal through his rage phase at low HP.

steps.text: Watch the story scene after defeating Dyle. Velvet spares him and the party regroups.
Exit Hadlow Hollow and return to Hellawes.

sections.title: Hellawes (2nd Visit)
sections.content:
## Hellawes (2nd Visit) — Confrontation with Teresa

Returning to Hellawes triggers a major boss confrontation. The Abbey’s exorcist **Teresa Linares** has set a trap for Velvet’s group. This visit is combat-heavy and marks the point where Laphicet officially joins as a permanent party member.

### Pre-Battle Warning

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes2-prep | Prepared for Teresa fight -->

**Prepare your party before entering the city.** A boss fight triggers automatically upon arrival:
- Equip the best available gear on all party members
- Ensure you have at least 10 Apple Gels and 5 Life Bottles
- Set up your Arte configurations for maximum damage output
- Consider equipping water resistance if available

### Boss: Teresa Linares

**Teresa** confronts the party with two tethered **Malakhim** supporting her.

- **HP:** 6,664
- **Supported by:** Two Malakhim (linked to boost her stats)
- **Element:** Water/Physical focused attacks
- **Strategy:**
  1. **Focus on defeating the Malakhim first** — they continuously boost Teresa’s attack and defense
  2. Once the Malakhim fall, Teresa becomes significantly weaker
  3. Use Velvet to corner Teresa against walls for extended combos
  4. Guard through her fast sword strikes — she has deceptive range
  5. Break Soul to heal through her water-elemental AoE attacks

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes2-teresa | Defeated Teresa -->

After the battle, important cutscenes play. **Laphicet** (the Malak boy spotted earlier) officially joins your party as a permanent member. He functions as your primary healer and ranged attacker — a crucial addition to the team.

### Post-Battle Activities

With Teresa defeated and Laphicet in your party:

1. **Restock at shops** — replace used healing items
2. **Rest at the inn** for skits — rest **twice** to trigger all available skits
3. **Equip Laphicet** — check his weapon, armor, and accessories
4. **Set Laphicet’s strategy** — configure him as a healer in the party strategy menu

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes2-restock | Restocked and rested -->

### Leaving Hellawes

When fully prepared, exit Hellawes toward the **West Laban Tunnel** — your path south toward the mainland.

<!-- checkpoint: hellawes2-exit | Left toward West Laban Tunnel -->

steps.text: Prepare your party before entering the city — a boss fight is triggered upon arrival.
**BOSS: Teresa** — HP: 6,664. Supported by two tethered Malakhim.
steps.note: Focus on defeating the Malakhim first — they boost Teresa’s stats. Use Velvet to corner her.

steps.text: **Laphicet** permanently joins your party after this battle. He serves as your primary healer and ranged attacker.
After defeating Teresa, watch the story cutscenes.
Open the party menu and fully equip Laphicet (weapon, armor, accessories).
Restock at shops and rest at the inn for skits.
steps.note: Rest twice to trigger all available skits.

steps.text: Exit Hellawes toward **West Laban Tunnel**.

sections.title: West Laban Tunnel
sections.content:
## West Laban Tunnel — Underground Passage South

The **West Laban Tunnel** connects the northern region (Hellawes area) to the southern mainland. This cave dungeon features branching paths, environmental hazards, and enemies that can inflict troublesome status ailments. With Laphicet in your party, you now have a proper four-member team.

### Enemies & Hazards

Common enemies in this area:
- **Rogue Rats** — fast, attack in groups
- **Ant-Lions** — earth-elemental, can cause paralysis
- **Bat-type Daemons** — flying enemies, harder to combo
- **Water Elementals** — magic-focused, stay at range

**Status ailments** (Poison and Paralysis) are common here. Equip accessories that resist these, or keep Panacea Bottles ready.

<!-- checkpoint: laban-entrance | Entered West Laban Tunnel -->

### Exploration

The tunnel is fairly linear but has several hidden nooks and side corridors:

**Entrance area:**
- **Life Bottle** — Basic chest near the entrance
- **Apple Gel** — Sparkle point on the ground

**First fork (left path):**
- **Amber Fragment** — Chest behind a breakable wall
- Search for sparkle points along the walls

**Water channel room (west):**
- **Verbena** — Hidden in a nook (permanent stat boost)
- Cross the rope bridge area for an **Amber Garment** chest

**Large circular room:**
- **Drop Earring** — Chest on a raised platform (requires jumping from the upper path)
- **Life Bottle** — Along the outer edge

<!-- checkpoint: laban-treasures | Collected tunnel treasures -->

### Katz Box

A **Katz Box** is located in a side passage in the central tunnel area. It requires **15 Katz Spirits** to open and typically contains a Katz Costume cosmetic item.

### Lower Flooded Cavern

The lower section has flooded passages:
- **Panacea Bottle** — In a side alcove
- **Lavender** — Dead end sparkle (permanent stat boost)
- **Amber Belt** — Box in a side alcove near the save point

<!-- checkpoint: laban-lower | Cleared the lower cavern -->

### Exit

After exploring thoroughly, continue through to the tunnel exit. The path leads to the **Burnack Plateau** — an open area with views of the sea and the fortress of Vortigern in the distance.

<!-- checkpoint: laban-exit | Exited to Burnack Plateau -->

steps.text: Enter the West Laban Tunnel and fight through enemy encounters.
Enemies here inflict Poison and Paralysis. Equip resistance accessories or keep Panacea Bottles ready.
Collect **Life Bottle** and **Apple Gel** near the entrance.
Find the **Amber Fragment** behind a breakable wall on the left fork.
Explore side paths for treasure chests — the tunnel has several branches.
steps.note: Check the rope bridge area for an Amber Garment and the raised platform for a Drop Earring.

steps.text: Open the **Katz Box** in the central tunnel side passage (costs 15 Katz Spirits).
Collect **Verbena**, **Lavender**, and **Amber Belt** from the lower flooded section.
Progress through the tunnel to reach the exit toward **Burnack Plateau**.

sections.title: Burnack Plateau
sections.content:
## Burnack Plateau — Open Fields Before Vortigern

The **Burnack Plateau** is a wide-open area between the West Laban Tunnel and the fortress of **Vortigern**. After the confined tunnels, this area offers sweeping views of the coastline and distant landmarks. It’s also an excellent grinding spot if your party is underleveled.

### Arrival

Exiting the West Laban Tunnel, jump down the cliffs to descend to the plateau floor. A cutscene plays showing the massive wall of **Vortigern** in the distance — your next major objective.

<!-- checkpoint: burnack-arrival | Arrived at Burnack Plateau -->

### Exploration & Items

The plateau is relatively straightforward but has items along its paths:

- **Verbena** — On the cliff path during descent (permanent stat boost)
- **Life Bottle** — Chest near the river at the bottom
- Several Katz Souls floating in the open fields
- Side paths lead to additional sparkle items

The enemies here are moderate in difficulty — a good balance for your current party level. If you’re finding upcoming content difficult, spend some time fighting here.

<!-- checkpoint: burnack-items | Collected plateau items -->

### Grinding Opportunity

This area is recommended for level grinding because:
- Enemies give decent XP relative to time investment
- The save point is nearby for HP restoration
- Enemy variety helps you practice different combat strategies
- Equipment mastery progresses quickly with frequent battles

Aim for around **level 18–20** before attempting Vortigern if you’re struggling.

### Party Setup

With a full four-member party (Velvet, Rokurou, Magilou, Laphicet), this is a good time to:
- Experiment with different party formations
- Set up AI strategies for each character
- Practice Switch Blast timing
- Configure Arte shortcut loadouts

<!-- checkpoint: burnack-grinding | Leveled up on the plateau -->

### Moving Forward

Continue east along the main path toward **Vortigern**. You’ll encounter **Eizen** here — a Malak and first mate of the pirate ship Van Eltia. A cutscene introduces him and his plan to help your party breach the Vortigern fortress.

<!-- checkpoint: burnack-exit | Headed to Vortigern -->

steps.text: Cross the open plateau area, fighting enemies along the paths.
Collect **Verbena** on the cliff descent and a **Life Bottle** from the chest near the river.
This is a good area to grind levels if you’re underleveled for upcoming fights. Aim for level 18–20.
Explore side paths for collectibles and Katz Souls.
Good time to experiment with party strategies and Arte configurations for the full 4-member team.
Continue east toward **Vortigern**. Meet **Eizen** in a story cutscene.

sections.title: Vortigern
sections.content:
## Vortigern — Fortress Infiltration

**Vortigern** is a massive fortress that controls sea passage between the northern and southern regions. The party must infiltrate it from the land side and open the sea gates so the pirate ship **Van Eltia** can pass through. **Eizen**, a Malak and the ship’s first mate, joins your party permanently for this mission.

### Meeting Eizen

Before entering the fortress, Eizen (known as "The Reaper" due to his cursed luck) explains the plan:
- The front gate is protected by a barrier — you can’t enter directly
- Instead, approach from the less-defended left side entrance
- Fight through the interior, reach the upper floors, and activate the gate switches
- The Van Eltia will sail through once the gates open

<!-- checkpoint: vortigern-eizen | Eizen joined the party -->

**Eizen permanently joins the party** after this scene. He’s a powerful physical brawler with earth-elemental Artes and excellent stagger potential. Equip him immediately.

### Entering the Fortress

Head left to find the less-defended entrance. The guards here transform into daemons — defeat them to enter. Inside, Vortigern is a winding, multi-level fortress filled with soldiers and daemons.

### Fortress Interior

Navigate through the fortress corridors:
- **Ground floor:** Linear path with guard encounters. Check side rooms for items
- **Earth Ring** — Chest in a side room on the ground floor
- **Life Bottles** — Multiple chests scattered throughout
- Use vines and windows to bypass locked doors when available
- **Verbena, Lavender** — Herb pickups along the path

<!-- checkpoint: vortigern-interior | Explored fortress interior -->

### Boss: Longsword Praetor

Midway through the fortress, you face the **Longsword Praetor** with two summoned Malakhim.

- **Strategy:** Take out the Malak links first to weaken the Praetor, then focus all damage on him
- Use Eizen’s heavy stagger attacks to interrupt the Praetor’s combos
- Laphicet should focus on healing the party

<!-- checkpoint: vortigern-praetor | Defeated the Longsword Praetor -->

### Opening the Gates

After the boss:
1. Return to the central room
2. Use newly accessible paths to reach the gate lever controls
3. Activate the levers to open the sea gates
4. The Van Eltia sails through during a cutscene
5. Fight a giant daemonized guard during the escape sequence

<!-- checkpoint: vortigern-gates | Opened the sea gates -->

### Escape & Boarding the Van Eltia

After opening the gates and defeating the final guard, the party escapes to the Van Eltia. Important story moments:
- **Laphicet officially receives his name** from Velvet during this sequence
- The party is now fully assembled for the journey south
- The Van Eltia becomes your mobile base for fast travel

### On the Van Eltia

Explore the ship for items and talk to all party members for skits:
- Check the cabins for equipment and items
- Talk to the pirate crew members for world-building
- Access **Expeditions** — a mini-game unlocked on the ship
- The ship’s shop has new equipment available

<!-- checkpoint: vortigern-ship | Boarded the Van Eltia -->

Set sail toward **Port Zekson** when ready.

steps.text: Meet Eizen and learn the infiltration plan for Vortigern fortress.
**Eizen permanently joins the party.** Equip him immediately — he’s a powerful physical brawler with earth Artes.
Enter the fortress through the left side entrance after defeating the daemon guards.
Navigate through the fortress interior, collecting items and fighting enemies.
steps.note: Check side rooms for Earth Ring, Life Bottles, and herb pickups.

steps.text: **BOSS: Longsword Praetor** — Supported by two Malakhim.
steps.note: Defeat Malak links first to weaken the Praetor. Use Eizen’s stagger attacks.

steps.text: Activate the gate lever controls to open the sea gates for the Van Eltia.
Defeat the giant daemonized guard during the escape sequence.
**Laphicet officially receives his name** from Velvet during the escape. Major story moment.
Explore the Van Eltia — talk to party members for skits, check cabins for items.
steps.note: Expeditions mini-game unlocks here. Ship shop has new equipment.

steps.text: Set sail toward **Port Zekson**.

sections.title: Port Zekson
sections.content:
## Port Zekson — Southern Harbor Town

**Port Zekson** is the first major town on the southern continent. After the long journey from the frozen north, this bustling port offers new shops, side content, and story developments. The Abbey’s presence is strong here, adding tension to your exploration.

### Arrival

Disembark from the Van Eltia and watch the arrival cutscenes. The party discusses their next moves and objectives — primarily tracking down information about **Artorius** and the Abbey’s plans.

<!-- checkpoint: zekson-arrival | Arrived at Port Zekson -->

### Town Exploration

Explore the port thoroughly:

**Dock Area:**
- **Apple Gel** — Bronze chest near the ship
- **Chocolate Idol** (Magilou equipment) — Silver chest left of the south exit
- Talk to the pirate by the Van Eltia for a bonus skit

**Town Proper:**
- Various NPCs provide information about the Abbey’s activities
- Green exclamation mark NPCs have important dialogue
- Several cooking ingredients available from sparkle points

<!-- checkpoint: zekson-explore | Explored Port Zekson -->

### Shopping

Visit the weapons vendor for significant upgrades:
- New weapons available for all party members (including Eizen)
- Armor improvements over anything found in the north
- Stock up on healing items for the road ahead
- Sell spare equipment and materials for Gald

### Inn & Skits

Rest at the inn to trigger available skits. These provide:
- Character development between party members
- Hints about upcoming story events
- Worldbuilding about the southern continent

<!-- checkpoint: zekson-rest | Rested and watched skits -->

### Moving On

When you’ve finished shopping and exploring, exit Port Zekson toward the **Danann Highway** — the main road leading to the capital city of Loegres.

<!-- checkpoint: zekson-exit | Left toward Danann Highway -->

steps.text: Arrive at Port Zekson and explore the port town.
Collect **Apple Gel** from the bronze chest near the ship.
Open the silver chest left of the south exit for the **Chocolate Idol** (Magilou equipment).
Talk to NPCs and gather information about the **Abbey’s** presence.
Visit shops to upgrade equipment for the party.
steps.note: Significant upgrades available for all party members including Eizen.

steps.text: Rest at the inn for available skits.
Exit toward **Danann Highway** when ready.

sections.title: Danann Highway
sections.content:
## Danann Highway — Road to the Capital

The **Danann Highway** is the main road connecting Port Zekson to the imperial capital of **Loegres**. It’s a wide, open area with rolling fields, scattered enemies, and plenty of hidden items off the beaten path. The scenery shifts from coastal to inland as you progress.

### Route Overview

The highway runs roughly north-south with several side areas branching off. Take your time exploring — the detours contain valuable equipment and permanent stat-boosting herbs.

<!-- checkpoint: danann-start | Started traversing Danann Highway -->

### Items & Collectibles

**Northeast corner (open area):**
- **Calcite Waistcoat** — Armor for male characters (good defense upgrade)

**Southeast path:**
- **Katz Box** — Hidden behind a large tree
- **Chamomile** — Herb pickup nearby (permanent stat boost)

**Eastern detour:**
- **Rosemary** — Permanent stat boost herb

**Northern side path:**
- **750 Gald** — Chest at the end of a dead-end path

**Western area:**
- **Earth Ring** — Accessory providing earth resistance

<!-- checkpoint: danann-items | Collected highway items -->

### Enemies

Enemies along the highway are moderate difficulty — slightly tougher than Burnack Plateau but not as dangerous as dungeon encounters. They provide good experience for maintaining appropriate levels before Loegres.

Watch for:
- Groups that can surround your party in the open fields
- Flying enemies that require aerial-targeting Artes
- Occasional stronger "elite" enemies that roam fixed paths

### Approaching Loegres

As you near the end of the highway, the capital city of **Loegres** becomes visible in the distance — a massive walled city that serves as the seat of the Holy Midgand Empire and the Abbey’s power center.

<!-- checkpoint: danann-exit | Reached Loegres -->

steps.text: Traverse the highway fighting enemies along the road.
Find the **Calcite Waistcoat** in the northeast corner of the open area.
Open the **Katz Box** behind the big tree and collect **Chamomile** nearby.
Explore side paths and open fields for hidden chests.
steps.note: Detour east for Rosemary, north for 750 Gald, and west for an Earth Ring.

steps.text: Collect **Rosemary** (east), **750 Gald** (north), and **Earth Ring** (west) from detour paths.
Continue along the highway toward **Loegres**, the capital city.

sections.title: Loegres (1st Visit)
sections.content:
## Loegres — Capital of the Holy Midgand Empire

**Loegres** is the largest city in the game and the seat of power for both the Holy Midgand Empire and the Abbey. This sprawling capital has multiple districts, numerous shops with significantly better equipment, side quests, and important story triggers. Plan to spend considerable time here.

### Arrival

Entering Loegres triggers cutscenes establishing the political situation. The Abbey’s influence is everywhere — exorcists patrol the streets and propaganda promotes Artorius as a hero. Your party must move carefully.

<!-- checkpoint: loegres-arrival | Arrived in Loegres -->

### City Exploration

**Near the entrance:**
- Sub-events available (look for exclamation marks)
- Start of "Magilou’s Menagerie in Motion" skit series

**By the Weapon Shop:**
- **Amber Paper** — Chest near the shop entrance

**Tavern area:**
- Speak to **Tabatha** to advance the main story
- "Search for a Missing Man" side quest begins here
- Code Red bounties available from the board

### Bloodwing Quests

Visit the guild to unlock **Bloodwing Quests** — these are bounty board side quests that send you to defeat specific powerful enemies. Rewards include:
- Rare equipment
- Unique materials
- Significant Gald payouts
- New quests unlock as you progress the story

<!-- checkpoint: loegres-guild | Unlocked Bloodwing Quests -->

### Shopping

Loegres shops have **significantly better gear** than anything available previously:
- New weapon tiers for all party members
- Major armor upgrades
- Powerful accessories
- Crafting materials

Spend your Gald here — the power jump is substantial and will help considerably in upcoming dungeons.

<!-- checkpoint: loegres-shops | Upgraded equipment -->

### Inn & Skits

Rest at the inn to trigger multiple skits:
- Party interactions about being in the capital
- Reactions to the Abbey’s propaganda
- Character backstory revelations
- Hints about upcoming objectives

### Story Progression

After exploring and shopping:
1. Speak to Tabatha in the tavern
2. Return to the docks for a plot scene
3. The party’s objectives become clear — investigate the **Barona Catacombs** beneath the city

<!-- checkpoint: loegres-story | Story objectives set -->

When the story objectives are established, head to the **Barona Catacombs** entrance (accessible from the cathedral area).

<!-- checkpoint: loegres-catacombs | Headed to Barona Catacombs -->

steps.text: Loegres is the capital of the Holy Midgand Empire. Many shops, side events, and story triggers here.
Explore the city and talk to NPCs for information about **Artorius** and the Abbey.
Find the **Amber Paper** in the chest near the weapon shop.
Visit the guild to unlock **Bloodwing Quests** (bounty board side quests).
steps.note: These provide rare equipment, materials, and Gald. New quests unlock as you progress.

steps.text: Upgrade equipment at the Loegres shops — they have significantly better gear.
steps.note: Major power jump available here. Spend your Gald on weapon and armor upgrades for all members.

steps.text: Rest at the inn and watch all available skits.
Speak to Tabatha in the tavern and return to the docks to advance the story.
Head to the **Barona Catacombs** when story objectives are set.

sections.title: Barona Catacombs
sections.content:
## Barona Catacombs — Beneath the Capital

The **Barona Catacombs** are an extensive underground network beneath Loegres. This dungeon features lever-based water drainage puzzles, tight corridors with ambush encounters, a challenging Code Red boss, and valuable treasures hidden throughout the flooded passages.

### Entrance

Speak to the soldier in the red scarf at the cathedral area in Loegres to gain access. A brief dialogue scene plays before you descend into the catacombs.

<!-- checkpoint: catacombs-entrance | Entered the Barona Catacombs -->

### Water Drainage Puzzles

The catacombs use a **lever-based drainage system** — pulling levers drains water from specific sections, opening new paths:

1. **First lever (south):** Drains the initial flooded area
2. Head east after draining for a **Verbena** herb
3. Continue south for a skit trigger and **Apple Gel** in the large room
4. Break the boulder blocking the next passage and grab a **Life Bottle**

### Mid-Dungeon Exploration

- Crawl through the eastern tunnel
- **Fork paths:** East leads to the Code Red boss, south continues the main path
- Collect **Wind Ring** after using another drainage lever
- Head upstairs for a **Katz Box**
- Southwest route: **Lavender** (permanent stat boost)
- Southeast: **Calcite Fragment**

<!-- checkpoint: catacombs-puzzles | Solved drainage puzzles -->

### Code Red Boss: Pillbug

In the eastern tunnels, you’ll encounter the **Pillbug** — a Code Red monster boss.

- **HP:** 21,581
- **Weakness:** Wind
- **Attacks:** Paralyzing non-elemental strikes that can break your Blast Souls gauge
- **Strategy:**
  - Use Wind-based Artes (Eizen’s earth won’t help here — rely on Velvet and Magilou)
  - Avoid his spinning moves — they have wide AoE
  - Manage your gauge carefully — Pillbug specifically targets it
  - Keep healing consistent; this is the toughest Code Red so far
  - Be patient — this is a war of attrition

<!-- checkpoint: catacombs-pillbug | Defeated Pillbug (Code Red) -->

### Final Section

After the Pillbug (or bypassing him if you prefer to return later):
- Use the final lever to drain the last water section
- Head southwest through a crawl tunnel
- Collect **880 Gald** from the chest at the end
- The exit leads toward the **Loegres Villa**

<!-- checkpoint: catacombs-exit | Completed Barona Catacombs -->

### Returning to Loegres

After completing the catacombs, briefly return to Loegres to:
- Restock healing items
- Save your progress
- Watch any newly available skits at the inn

steps.text: Enter the catacombs via the cathedral area (speak to the soldier in the red scarf).
The catacombs have dead-end paths with strong enemies — save frequently.
Pull the first lever (south) to drain the flooded area and open new paths.
Collect **Verbena** (east), **Apple Gel** (south room), and **Life Bottle** (past the boulder).
Explore all branches for treasure chests and equipment.
steps.note: Find the Wind Ring, Katz Box (upstairs), Lavender (southwest), and Calcite Fragment (southeast).

steps.text: **CODE RED BOSS: Pillbug** — HP: 21,581. Weak to Wind.
steps.note: Paralyzing attacks that break Blast Souls gauge. Use Wind Artes and be patient — war of attrition.

steps.text: Collect **880 Gald** from the chest in the southwest crawl tunnel.
Reach the deepest chamber and complete the story objective.
Return to Loegres after completing the catacombs. Restock and save.

sections.title: Loegres Villa
sections.content:
## Loegres Villa — Infiltrating the Abbey’s Stronghold

The **Loegres Villa** is an Abbey-controlled mansion that your party must infiltrate as part of the main story. This section features tense stealth-adjacent exploration, multiple combat encounters with guards, missable equipment in side rooms, and culminates in significant boss fights.

### Entering the Villa

After the Barona Catacombs, the story directs you to infiltrate the Villa. Watch the introductory cutscene that establishes the stakes — key Abbey members are meeting here, and your party needs information.

<!-- checkpoint: villa-entrance | Entered Loegres Villa -->

### Villa Exploration

Navigate counterclockwise through the villa, checking every room:

**Ground Floor:**
- **Panacea Bottle** — First side room on the left
- **Rosemary** — Herb in the dining hall area
- **Wind Ring** — Chest in a storage closet

**Upper Floor:**
- **Saffron** — Herb on the upper corridor
- Additional equipment in bedrooms

<!-- checkpoint: villa-items | Collected villa items -->

### Important Warning

**Search ALL rooms before progressing through the main hall door.** Several rooms contain missable equipment and items that become inaccessible after triggering the story confrontation. The villa does not allow backtracking after certain story triggers.

### Story Confrontation

Reaching the main hall triggers the story confrontation. Cutscenes reveal important plot information about the Abbey’s plans and Artorius’s true objectives. After the dialogue, combat begins.

<!-- checkpoint: villa-confrontation | Triggered the main hall confrontation -->

### Boss: Eleanor (2nd Battle)

Before reaching Oscar, you fight **Eleanor Hume** again:

- **HP:** 16,139
- **Weakness:** Water
- **Strategy:**
  - She has backup support — defeat her allies first
  - Use water-elemental attacks once she’s isolated
  - At 50% HP, **Magilou joins** with a Switch Blast tutorial
  - Use Switch Blast when prompted for massive damage
  - After learning Switch Blast, finish Eleanor aggressively

### Boss: Oscar Dragonia

The main boss of this section is **Oscar Dragonia** — a high-ranking exorcist.

- **Weakness:** Susceptible to status ailments
- **Strategy:**
  - Use attacks and Mystic Artes that inflict ailments
  - Guard against his powerful Arte combos — damage spikes are sudden
  - Use Laphicet for consistent healing support
  - Break Soul when Oscar staggers for extended combo windows
  - Keep Life Bottles ready — his burst damage can KO unexpectedly

<!-- checkpoint: villa-oscar | Defeated Oscar Dragonia -->

### Post-Battle

After defeating Oscar:
1. Watch the important post-battle cutscenes
2. The party regroups and discusses next steps
3. Return to the tavern area to heal and save
4. Open a **Katz Box** in the villa basement before leaving

<!-- checkpoint: villa-complete | Completed Loegres Villa -->

This concludes the first major arc of Tales of Berseria. The story continues with new destinations and escalating conflicts against the Abbey.

steps.text: Infiltrate the **Loegres Villa** as part of the main story.
Navigate through the villa corridors counterclockwise, defeating guards along the way.
Search all rooms before progressing — several contain missable equipment and items. No backtracking after story triggers.
Collect **Panacea Bottle**, **Rosemary**, **Wind Ring**, and **Saffron** from villa rooms.
Reach the main hall and trigger the story confrontation.
**BOSS: Eleanor** — HP: 16,139. Weak to Water.
steps.note: Defeat allies first, then use water Artes. Magilou joins at 50% HP with Switch Blast tutorial.

steps.text: **BOSS: Oscar Dragonia** — Exorcist-class enemy vulnerable to status ailments.
steps.note: Use Laphicet for healing. Break Soul when Oscar staggers. Watch for burst damage spikes.

steps.text: Open the **Katz Box** in the villa basement before leaving.
Watch the post-battle cutscenes and regroup with the party at the tavern.
